disallowed_inputs: all files not listed in allowed_inputs

checks.name: risk-workflow-match
checks.evidence: T-001 through T-005 are high-risk integrity or gate changes and require tdd; T-006 is a medium-risk reversible release-metadata change and requires acceptance-first. These assignments match the risk classification policy and risk-gate matrix.

checks.name: executable-evidence
checks.evidence: Every high-risk task requires Red-to-Green evidence at a task-specific verification path, independent review, spec revision and environment provenance, changed-file hashes, and requirement-traceability proof. T-002 additionally gives exact Bash, PowerShell, and parity commands; T-006 names repository and install/release validation outcomes.

checks.name: exact-planned-paths
checks.evidence: Every Planned Files entry is repository-qualified. T-002 intentionally qualifies the fixture-tree scope as tests/fixtures/workflow-state/, while all scripts, contracts, reports, manifests, marketplaces, workflows, and test entry points are named by exact repository-relative paths.

checks.name: semantic-acceptance-ownership
checks.evidence: Acceptance ownership follows implementation semantics: T-002 owns AC-001/002/006/011, T-003 owns AC-003/004/005/007/008/014, T-004 owns AC-009, T-005 owns AC-010/012, and T-006 owns AC-013.

checks.name: dependency-and-overlap-safety
checks.evidence: The executable order is acyclic: T-001 and T-002 precede T-003; T-003 precedes T-004 and T-005; both precede T-006. Overlaps are ordered safely: T-001 repairs downstream precheck scripts/tests before T-005 integrates validation into them, and T-004 updates repository validation before T-006 updates its release constants.

checks.name: rollback-planning
checks.evidence: Each task defines a bounded rollback unit and an inspectable post-rollback validation or isolated-fixture exercise. Registry/history rollback explicitly avoids mutating historical artifacts, and validator/integration rollback preserves prerequisite controls.

checks.name: diagnostic-path
checks.evidence: The bugfix task T-001 preserves the required symptom-to-root-cause-to-fix-to-regression path: reproduce the Spec-Passed transition failure, diagnose canonical hash/path mismatch, apply narrow normalization, and retain traversal, stale-hash, escape, and forgery regressions.

checks.name: precheck-and-structural-context
checks.evidence: precheck-result.json reports workflow_match_precheck PASS and valid blockers format. integrated-summary.json reports nine reviewer-A PASS checks and no FAIL or SKIP checks; no duplicate structural finding is emitted.

checks.name: round-2-recheck-t002-exact-red-green
checks.evidence: T-002 names the exact pre-implementation command bash tests/workflow-state-registry.tests.sh and red path specs/workflow-state-integrity/verification/T-002.red.log; it names exact post-implementation Bash, PowerShell, and parity commands and green path specs/workflow-state-integrity/verification/T-002.green.log.

checks.name: round-2-recheck-fully-qualified-planned-files
checks.evidence: All six tasks use repository-relative qualified paths for every Planned Files entry; no bare filename or ambiguous basename remains.

checks.name: round-2-recheck-ac010-owned-by-t005
checks.evidence: T-005 explicitly declares Requirements: REQ-008, REQ-010; AC-010, AC-012 and its Scope and Done When clauses cover full quality-gate invocation plus scoped Impl/Task precheck invocation while retaining predecessor checks.
